why_them: Site is built on GoDaddy Website Builder — a platform with limited SEO capability and poor extensibility. No integrated online booking system; appointment calls route to a phone number only. Testimonials section exists but contains no visible patient reviews. No blog, no Google Reviews widget, and no structured service pages to support local SEO.
recommended_service: Website rebuild with integrated online booking and local SEO
outreach_hook: Your GoDaddy site has no real booking system — patients searching for a Dumfries optician can't reserve an appointment without picking up the phone.

why_them: The Webflow site is well-designed with MySight booking integration — above average for an independent practice in D&G. The Lockerbie branch operates under the same brand but is not represented on the website, creating a local SEO blind spot for that market. No FAQ section, no pricing transparency, and minimal team bios.
recommended_service: SEO expansion and Lockerbie branch landing page
outreach_hook: Your Lockerbie branch has no web presence — anyone in Lockerbie searching for an optician won't find you.

why_them: The site runs on Drupal — an ageing, developer-heavy CMS rarely used for small independent practices, meaning updates likely require technical help or are simply not happening. Design is functional but dated compared to modern optician competitors. No patient testimonials or Google Reviews integration visible. The booking page exists but appears basic with no real-time availability system.
recommended_service: Website redesign with modern CMS and local SEO for two branches
outreach_hook: Your Drupal website is hard to update and already looks dated next to competitors — it's likely costing you new patients in Kirkcudbright and Lockerbie.

why_them: Purcell Opticians has a well-constructed Webflow site with MySight booking, video backgrounds, and clear service pages — one of the stronger independent optician websites in D&G. The main gaps are no live pricing information, no team bios, and no blog or SEO content strategy to compete for wider Kirkcudbrightshire searches.
recommended_service: SEO content strategy and Google Ads campaign
outreach_hook: Your site looks great but has almost no blog or SEO content — competitors could overtake you in Castle Douglas search results without it.

why_them: D Little Opticians has been trading for over 30 years in Castle Douglas with no website at all — only directory listings and no findable email address. The domain returned a DNS failure, confirming there is no live online presence. In a town where a competitor is at the same address range with a modern site, D Little is invisible to any patient searching online.
recommended_service: New website build with online booking and local SEO
outreach_hook: After 30 years in Castle Douglas, D Little Opticians has no website — every patient who searches online finds your competitor down the street instead.

location: 2 Bath Place, High Street, Moffat, DG10 9HJ
why_them: The WordPress site is functional but noticeably dated, with no social media links, no testimonials, and no Google Reviews widget. A COVID-19 guidance page still visible in navigation signals the site has not been meaningfully updated in years. The practice offers specialist dry eye and glaucoma clinics that are not prominently marketed, representing a missed opportunity for targeted local SEO.
recommended_service: Website refresh, local SEO and specialist clinic landing pages
outreach_hook: Your dry eye and glaucoma clinics deserve their own pages — right now patients searching for those specialist services in Moffat find nothing.

why_them: Built on GoDaddy Website Builder with a placeholder GoDaddy email still exposed in the page source — suggesting the owner set it up themselves and has not fully configured it. No business email address is publicly findable anywhere online. The shop focuses on distinctive frames but has no frame finder, no lens ordering tool, and limited product filtering to support e-commerce.
recommended_service: Website rebuild with proper e-commerce, SEO and contact setup
outreach_hook: Your GoDaddy site still has a placeholder email in its code — customers searching for a contact address for The Wee Spec Shop find nothing.

why_them: Peter Foulds Opticians has been trading independently in Newton Stewart for nearly 30 years with no website — only NHS and Yell directory listings. No email address is findable anywhere online. Newton Stewart serves a wide rural catchment across Wigtownshire, yet this practice is essentially invisible to anyone who does not already know it exists.
recommended_service: New website build with local SEO and online booking
outreach_hook: Nearly 30 years serving Newton Stewart and the Machars — yet anyone searching online for an optician there has no way to find you.
